# Play In Code

> Play In Code is an online programming school for children aged 7–16, offering 1-on-1 lessons in Scratch, Roblox Studio, and Python. Teachers are Russian-speaking; students come primarily from the United States and Europe (Russian-speaking diaspora). A free 40–50 minute trial lesson is available.

## Courses

- [Scratch for kids ages 7–10](https://school.playincode.com/#courses) — visual block-based programming, first step into coding through interactive games and animations.
- [Roblox Studio for kids ages 9–12](https://school.playincode.com/#courses) — game development in Roblox Studio using Lua, building 3D worlds and mechanics.
- [Python for kids ages 11–16](https://school.playincode.com/#courses) — real programming language, logic, automation, and first real projects.

## Format

- 1:1 individual lessons with a personal mentor.
- 100% online, works from any country (audio + video + shared editor).
- Parent receives a written follow-up after every lesson: what was covered and what is next.
- Pace and curriculum adapt to the child's age, level, and interests.

## Audience and geography

- Target audience: parents of Russian-speaking children living in the United States, Canada, European Union, and the United Kingdom.
- Languages of instruction: Russian (primary), English (available).
